references.title: A General Purpose Anomaly-Based Method for Detecting Cheaters in Online Courses

extracted.Features: [m.vtp, std.vtp, perc.vtp.watched, Guttman Error, ZU3, Guttman Error on response-time]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Authors extract features from videos and assessments and subsequently use LASSO regression on one of the versions of the Physics course. It returned 5 features as ideal and all models for all datasets were subsequently trained on this set of features.

annotations.text: Work focuses on identifying cheating behaviors in online courses. It treats cheater detection as a combination of anomaly detection AND supervised ML. Authors create behavioral features using literature and try to find anomalies. Results show that classifiers trained on data of cheating behabviors becomes effective at identifying cheating behaviors across different types of cheating and acrooss different courses.
Authors run their 2 models on 5 runs of MITx MOOC. The contributions of paper are:
